title: Whole-cell proteomics and Analysis by Tandem Mass Tagging-based proteomics
author: Felix Kraus
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bxa4pigw

steps.instruction: For whole proteome analysis, 50 µg, of protein lysate is required for each replicate. Lyse cells in lysis buffer and pass them through a 21G needle 10 times. Alternatively, lyse cells by sonication as per manufactures instructions.
Centrifugate suspensions at 13000 rpm, 4 °C, , (high speed) for 00:10:00 and collect the supernatant.

Transfer quantified protein lysate concentration and 50 µg, of lysate for each sample to a clean 1.5 mL protein Lo-Bind Eppendorf tubes. Reduce lysates for 00:20:00 at Room temperature with 5 millimolar (mM), TCEP , and alkylate cysteine residues with 20 millimolar (mM), Chloroacetamide ( Room temperature , 00:30:00 ).
Extract protein content by methanol-chloroform precipitation and subsequent MeOH washes.
Add 4x volumes of MeOH and vortex.
Resuspend protein pellets 100 µL of 200 millimolar (mM), EPPS buffer ( pH 8.5 ).
Digest samples at 37 °C for 02:00:00 with endoproteinase Lys-C (Wako, Japan) at a 1/200 enzyme/protein ratio.
Digest with Trypsin (1:100) for 06:00:00 at 37 °C .

Add 5 µL, of the TMT reagent to each sample. Solubilize TMT reagents are in ACN as per manufacturer’s instructions and 5 µL, of TMT label is used for every 50 µg, of protein lysate . Performe TMT labeling in a final concentration of 20-25% ACN. Add 20 µL, of ACN to bring the reaction volume at 125 µL.The number of samples, and hence the number of individual TMT reagents, will depend upon the design of the experiment.
Incubate for 01:00:00 at Room temperature to label the samples.
Add 1x volume of chloroform and vortex.
Add 3x volume of water and vortex.
Spin down at Room temperature for 00:05:00 at high speed.
Aspirate and discard the upper aqueous phase. Do not disturb the protein disc at the interface of the aqueous phase (top) and organic phase (bottom).
Add 4x volumes of MeOH and vortex.
Spin down at Room temperature for 00:05:00 at high speed.
Air dry (or speed-vac) protein pellet down to remove all traces of MeOH.
Repeat MeOH wash.
Aspirate and discard supernatant. Do not disturb the protein pellet at the bottom of the tube.

Combine 1% of each labeled sample together in a tube, quench the reaction with 4 µL, of 5% hydroxylamine for 00:15:00 at Room temperature , and dry down using the speed-vac. This combined sample is used to perform the ratio check to test labeling efficiency. The remaining amount of each sample can be stored in the freezer.
Resuspend the dried sample in 100 µL, of 5% FA/5% ACN . Check to ensure that the pH of the sample is ~ pH 3 (or lower) using pH strip.
Make stage tip by placing 6-8 “cookies” of C-18 embedded membranes in 200 µL, Rainin tip .
Perform C-18 cleanup:
Equilibrate C-18 with 100 µL, of 100% methanol .
Dry down eluted peptides in speed-vac.
Wash C-18 with 50 µL, of 50% ACN/5% FA .
Wash C-18 with 100 µL, of 5% ACN/5% FA .
Load sample on to C-18 to bind peptides.
Wash bound peptides on C-18 with 50 µL, of 5% ACN/5% FA .
Elute peptides off C-18 with 50 µL, 75% ACN/ 5% FA into a mass spec vial.
Reconstitute peptides in 10 µL, 5% ACN/5% FA .
Perform ratio check by analyzing the total amount of reporter ions present, as measured by mass spectrometry, for each TMT reporter ion channel.

Quench the entire volume of each sample using 8 µL, of 5% hydroxylamine for 00:15:00 at Room temperature . Combine samples in 2 ml Eppendorf tube to equal amounts, based on normalization values you obtain from the ratio check.
Dry down labeled, combined sample in speed-vac.
Re-constitute the sample in 750 µL, of 5% ACN/5% FA .
Place SepPak column into vacuum slot on a vacuum manifold.
Start the vacuum, gently, try to ensure that the pressure gauge is below 10. This can be achieved by opening one of the valves gently. The fluid should pass through slowly, drop wise.
Fill SepPak with 1 mL, 5% FA/50% ACN , Flow Liquid through.
Fill SepPak with 1 mL, 5% FA/5% ACN , Flow Liquid through, repeat 2 more times.
Replace tube with 2 ml collection tube.
Add Peptides, Flow Liquid through (~ 750 µl).
Wash with 1 mL, 5% FA/5% ACN , Flow Liquid through, 2 times.
Replace 15 ml collection tube with 2 ml collection tube.
Elute with 750 µL, 75% ACN/5% FA . Since there is residual liquid left in the SepPak, ensure that all the liquid flows through SepPak.
Dry down in SpeedVac Room temperature Overnight or 30 °C .
Fill SepPak with 1 mL, 100% MeOH .
Resuspend sample in 100 µL, of 10 mM NH4HCO3 pH 8 .
Fractionate using pH reverse-phase HPLC:
Fractionate samples by high-pH reverse-phase HPLC (Agilent LC1260) into 96 fractions over a 01:30:00 run.

Fractions are run through an Aeris peptide XB-c18 column (Phenomenex; 250 mm x 3.6 mm), with mobile phase A containing 5 %, ACN and 10 millimolar (mM), NH4HCO3 pH 8 and phase B containing 90 %, ACN and 10 millimolar (mM), NH4HCO3 pH 8 (all inLC-MS grade H 2 0).
Combine fractionated samples (either 12 or 24 fractions) in a non-continuous manner into individual 1.5 ml Eppendorf tube (see outline below form Paulo et al., 2016 ).
Dry down in SpeedVac.
Resuspend peptides in 100 µL, 5%FA /5% ACN .
Check pH (~3.5) with pH indicator strips.
Stage tip each fraction.
Make stage tips and equilibrate. Spin down at 3000 rpm, °C, 3 minutes .
Perform C-18 cleanup:
Wash with 50 µL, 50% ACN /5% FA .
Wash with 100 µL, 5% ACN /5% FA .
Load sample
Collect flow through and freeze.
Wash with 50 µL, 50% ACN /5% FA .
Elute with 50 µL, 75% ACN/1% FA in mass spec vial.
Dry down in SpeedVac.
Reconstitute pellet in 10 µL, 5% FA / 5% ACN .
Freeze sample at - -20 °C until ready to run proteomics.
